author: @MegaVolcano
text:
Let’s see if we can pick up the pattern here, shall we?
2 weeks (roughly) before premier of Obiwan media starts calling fans racist and sexist. Show airs, strong black woman character cannot act her way out of a paper bag, plot breaks lore, writing is mediocre, show generally sucks and drops in ratings.
2 weeks (roughly) before premier of Acolyte media and cast begin calling fans racist, homophobic, sexist, etc. Show airs, Strong black lesbian woman character cannot act her way out of a paper bag. Plot breaks lore, writing is horrible, show sucks so bad that the ratings fall off of a cliff. Show immediately cancelled.
2 weeks (roughly) before Andor S2 premiere media begins calling fans racist and sexist and toxic…
What happens next?

author: @gurugamer3196
text: I acknowledge that the situation regarding the Star Wars franchise is indeed a reflection of fan engagement. It is the fans and Disney customers who, through their purchasing decisions, ultimately determine the success or failure of a product. If the product fails to meet our expectations, we will refrain from making a purchase, leading to its decline. This does not alter the fundamental truth that a subpar product will not attract customers. Nonetheless, we collectively agree that the customer retains the final authority, having significant influence over whether a product succeeds or fails.

replies.author: @minabee369
replies.text:
My sister, three of my nephews, and I will be watching Revenge of the Sith this Sunday. The 20th anniversary re-release is is officially April 25th,  but it was almost impossible to reserve five seats together as Friday and Saturday showings were either sold out or almost there. I'll be also seeing it alone Thursday afternoon. 

Star Wars fandom is still alive, but just not in the zombieland that is Disney Star Wars.

And despite all the praise for Blandor, it's not going to save Disney Star Wars. It'll do better than whatever that Jude Law show was but it won't do Kenobi numbers let alone Mandalorian season 1 and 2.
